message:
fix(classes): class-level is_directed/is_multigraph for nx create_using=fnx (br-classpred)

nx.utils.misc.check_create_using does
``G.is_directed(None) if isinstance(G, type) else G.is_directed()``
when deciding whether a create_using CLASS matches expected
directedness. On nx, is_directed / is_multigraph are plain methods
that ignore self, so calling with self=None works. fnx's Rust
descriptors require a real instance and raised
``TypeError: descriptor 'is_directed' for 'franken_networkx.Graph'
objects doesn't apply to a 'NoneType' object``. This broke
nx.gnp_random_graph, nx.barabasi_albert_graph, and any nx generator
that accepts ``create_using=fnx.Graph``.

Fix: _make_class_safe_predicate wraps is_directed / is_multigraph so
class-level calls return the class-level default and instance-level
calls still delegate to the underlying Rust descriptor.

6 regression tests in TestClassLevelPredicates cover class/instance
paths + end-to-end nx generators with fnx create_using.

Test suite: 4149 -> 4152 passed, 0 regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -170,7 +170,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_networkx-69m","title":"Graph Products: corona, modular, rooted, lexicographic products","description":"Graph Products

Implementations of standard graph product operations that combine two graphs into a new graph.

## Functions to Implement

- `tensor_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Nodes are (g,h) pairs. Edge ((g1,h1),(g2,h2)) iff (g1,g2) in G.edges AND (h1,h2) in H.edges. Also called categorical/kronecker product.
- `cartesian_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Edge ((g1,h1),(g2,h2)) iff (g1==g2 AND (h1,h2) in H.edges) OR ((g1,g2) in G.edges AND h1==h2).
- `lexicographic_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Edge ((g1,h1),(g2,h2)) iff (g1,g2) in G.edges OR (g1==g2 AND (h1,h2) in H.edges).
- `strong_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Union of tensor and cartesian products.
- `corona_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Take G plus |G| copies of H, connect each node g_i to all nodes in H_i.
- `rooted_product(G, H, root) -> Graph`: Take |G| copies of H, identify root node of each H copy with corresponding G node.
- `power(G, k) -> Graph`: G^k where nodes are adjacent if distance ≤ k in G.

## Algorithmic Approach

- Product node space: iterate over itertools.product(G.nodes, H.nodes) for all products.
- Tensor: iterate all edge pairs from G and H.
- Cartesian: for each G edge, connect across all H nodes; for each H edge, connect across all G nodes.
- Power: BFS from each node up to depth k, add edges to all discovered nodes.
- Node attributes: merge from both factor graphs. Edge attributes: merge where applicable.

## Performance Targets

- Products of graphs with 1K nodes each (→ 1M product nodes): < 10 seconds.
- Power graph: O(n * (n + m)) using BFS from each node.

## Edge Cases

- Empty graph factors → empty product.
- Single-node factors.
- Self-loops in factor graphs.
- Directed vs undirected factor graphs (must match or raise error).
- Multigraph factors.
- Node attribute conflicts between G and H.
- power(G, 0) → no edges; power(G, 1) → G itself.
- corona_product with H being empty graph.

## Test Requirements

- Unit tests: verify node count = |G| * |H| for standard products, edge counts match formulas.
- Algebraic identity tests:
  - cartesian_product(G, H) isomorphic to cartesian_product(H, G) (commutativity).
  - tensor_product(G, H) isomorphic to tensor_product(H, G).
  - strong_product = union of tensor and cartesian edge sets.
- NX cross-validation: compare edge sets with NetworkX on small graphs (K3, P4, C5, Petersen).
- Property tests: product of connected graphs is connected for cartesian product.
- Attribute preservation tests: node/edge attributes from factor graphs survive in product.
- Known graph tests: cartesian_product(P2, P3) = grid_graph([2,3]).
- power(cycle(n), k) edge count verification.
- Round-trip tests: verify product graph properties (e.g., tensor product of bipartite graphs is bipartite).
- Stress tests: products of moderate-size graphs to verify performance targets.

## Implementation Notes

- Pure Python implementation; graph construction dominates runtime.
- Depends on core graph types, itertools, BFS (for power).
- Must preserve directed/undirected type from input graphs.
- Node naming convention: tuple (g, h) for product nodes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T02:19:47.612874210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:36:21.318196593Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T03:36:21.317579246Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 graph products: corona_product, modular_product, rooted_product, lexicographic_product. Total: 488 functions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","tier-3"]}

 {"id": "franken_networkx-grattrset", "title": "REVIEW: [HIGH] G.graph = {...} assignment raises AttributeError — breaks nx.subgraph_view", "description": "nx internals (subgraph_view, restricted_view, relabel) do `newG.graph = G.graph` but fnx Graph.graph is a read-only getset_descriptor. _GraphAttrsDescriptor adds a __set__ that clears+updates the existing dict in place.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "close_reason": "Fixed in cycle-8."}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-adjprivset", "title": "REVIEW: [HIGH] _node/_adj assignment AttributeError blocks nx.subgraph_view pipeline", "description": "After br-grattrset fixed the .graph setter, nx.subgraph_view then tries `newG._node = FilterAtlas(...)`. fnx._node is a read-only property. Making it settable alone is insufficient because fnx.Graph.__iter__ reads the Rust storage directly, bypassing any user-set override — so FilterAtlas-based filtering would not work. Proper fix requires Rust-side Graph to support external adjacency/node storage. Queued.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-adjcp", "title": "REVIEW: [HIGH] AdjacencyView lacks copy() — nx.to_dict_of_dicts breaks on fnx MultiGraph", "description": "Earlier br-atlascp added copy() to AtlasView. MultiGraph's inner adjacency uses AdjacencyView (G.adj[u] -> dict-of-keyed-attrs); it also lacked copy(), so nx.to_dict_of_dicts(fnx_multigraph) crashed with AttributeError: 'AdjacencyView' object has no attribute 'copy'. Added copy() to AdjacencyView (returns dict-of-dicts snapshot) and to MultiAdjacencyView (returns dict-of-dicts-of-dicts recursively).", "status": "closed", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T08:08:51.243295Z", "close_reason": "Fixed: AdjacencyView.copy() and MultiAdjacencyView.copy() return plain dict-snapshot structures. 3 regression tests cover nx.to_dict_of_dicts on fnx MultiGraph + direct copy() calls.", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T08:08:51.243295Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T08:08:51.243295Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id": "franken_networkx-classpred", "title": "REVIEW: [HIGH] Graph.is_directed(None) raises — nx generators with create_using=fnx.Graph fail", "description": "nx.utils.misc.check_create_using does `G.is_directed(None) if isinstance(G, type) else G.is_directed()` when deciding whether a create_using CLASS matches expected directedness. On nx, these are plain methods that ignore self, so self=None works. fnx's Rust descriptors require a real instance and raise TypeError: 'descriptor is_directed for franken_networkx.Graph objects doesn't apply to a NoneType object'. This broke nx.gnp_random_graph, nx.barabasi_albert_graph, and any nx generator with create_using=fnx.Graph. Fix: _make_class_safe_predicate wraps is_directed / is_multigraph so class-level calls return the class-level default and instance calls still delegate to the Rust descriptor.", "status": "closed", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T08:13:35.291148Z", "close_reason": "Fixed: class-level is_directed/is_multigraph wrappers applied to all 4 graph classes. 6 regression tests in TestClassLevelPredicates cover class/instance paths + end-to-end nx generators with fnx create_using.", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T08:13:35.291148Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T08:13:35.291148Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

@@ -1913,6 +1913,44 @@ def _graph_deepcopy(self, memo=None):
 MultiGraph.to_undirected_class = _to_undirected_class
 MultiDiGraph.to_directed_class = _to_directed_class
 MultiDiGraph.to_undirected_class = _to_undirected_class
+
+
+# br-classpred: nx.utils.misc.check_create_using does
+# ``G.is_directed(None) if isinstance(G, type) else G.is_directed()``
+# when deciding whether a create_using CLASS matches expected
+# directedness. On nx classes, is_directed / is_multigraph are plain
+# methods that ignore self, so calling them with self=None works. fnx's
+# Rust descriptors require a real instance and raise TypeError, which
+# breaks ``nx.gnp_random_graph(..., create_using=fnx.Graph)`` and any
+# nx generator that passes a fnx class as create_using. Wrap the Rust
+# methods so they can be called on a class (returning the class-level
+# default) OR on an instance (delegating to the Rust descriptor).
+def _make_class_safe_predicate(raw, class_default):
+    def predicate(self, *args, **kwargs):
+        if self is None or isinstance(self, type):
+            return class_default
+        return raw(self, *args, **kwargs)
+
+    return predicate
+
+
+_GRAPH_RAW_IS_DIRECTED = Graph.is_directed
+_GRAPH_RAW_IS_MULTIGRAPH = Graph.is_multigraph
+_DIGRAPH_RAW_IS_DIRECTED = DiGraph.is_directed
+_DIGRAPH_RAW_IS_MULTIGRAPH = DiGraph.is_multigraph
+_MULTIGRAPH_RAW_IS_DIRECTED = MultiGraph.is_directed
+_MULTIGRAPH_RAW_IS_MULTIGRAPH = MultiGraph.is_multigraph
+_MULTIDIGRAPH_RAW_IS_DIRECTED = MultiDiGraph.is_directed
+_MULTIDIGRAPH_RAW_IS_MULTIGRAPH = MultiDiGraph.is_multigraph
+
+Graph.is_directed = _make_class_safe_predicate(_GRAPH_RAW_IS_DIRECTED, False)
+Graph.is_multigraph = _make_class_safe_predicate(_GRAPH_RAW_IS_MULTIGRAPH, False)
+DiGraph.is_directed = _make_class_safe_predicate(_DIGRAPH_RAW_IS_DIRECTED, True)
+DiGraph.is_multigraph = _make_class_safe_predicate(_DIGRAPH_RAW_IS_MULTIGRAPH, False)
+MultiGraph.is_directed = _make_class_safe_predicate(_MULTIGRAPH_RAW_IS_DIRECTED, False)
+MultiGraph.is_multigraph = _make_class_safe_predicate(_MULTIGRAPH_RAW_IS_MULTIGRAPH, True)
+MultiDiGraph.is_directed = _make_class_safe_predicate(_MULTIDIGRAPH_RAW_IS_DIRECTED, True)
+MultiDiGraph.is_multigraph = _make_class_safe_predicate(_MULTIDIGRAPH_RAW_IS_MULTIGRAPH, True)
 Graph.adjacency = _simple_graph_adjacency
 DiGraph.adjacency = _simple_graph_adjacency
 MultiGraph.adjacency = _multigraph_adjacency

@@ -1185,3 +1185,62 @@ def test_multi_adj_view_copy_deep(self):
         MG.add_edge(0, 1, key=0, w=5)
         snapshot = MG.adj.copy()
         assert snapshot == {0: {1: {0: {"w": 5}}}, 1: {0: {0: {"w": 5}}}}
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regression: franken_networkx-classpred — is_directed/is_multigraph on class
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+class TestClassLevelPredicates:
+    """nx.utils.misc.check_create_using does
+    ``G.is_directed(None) if isinstance(G, type) else G.is_directed()``
+    when deciding whether a create_using CLASS matches expected
+    directedness. On nx, these are plain methods that ignore self, so
+    passing self=None works. fnx's Rust descriptors required a real
+    instance and raised TypeError. Wrapper makes is_directed /
+    is_multigraph callable on the class (returning the class-level
+    default) without breaking instance-level calls.
+    """
+
+    def test_class_level_is_directed(self):
+        assert fnx.Graph.is_directed(None) is False
+        assert fnx.DiGraph.is_directed(None) is True
+        assert fnx.MultiGraph.is_directed(None) is False
+        assert fnx.MultiDiGraph.is_directed(None) is True
+
+    def test_class_level_is_multigraph(self):
+        assert fnx.Graph.is_multigraph(None) is False
+        assert fnx.DiGraph.is_multigraph(None) is False
+        assert fnx.MultiGraph.is_multigraph(None) is True
+        assert fnx.MultiDiGraph.is_multigraph(None) is True
+
+    def test_instance_level_still_works(self):
+        G = fnx.Graph()
+        D = fnx.DiGraph()
+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()
+        MD = fnx.MultiDiGraph()
+        assert G.is_directed() is False
+        assert D.is_directed() is True
+        assert MG.is_multigraph() is True
+        assert MD.is_multigraph() is True
+
+    def test_nx_gnp_random_graph_with_fnx_create_using(self):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        g = nx.gnp_random_graph(10, 0.3, seed=42, create_using=fnx.Graph)
+        assert isinstance(g, fnx.Graph)
+        assert g.number_of_nodes() == 10
+
+    def test_nx_barabasi_albert_with_fnx_create_using(self):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        g = nx.barabasi_albert_graph(10, 2, seed=42, create_using=fnx.Graph)
+        assert isinstance(g, fnx.Graph)
+        assert g.number_of_nodes() == 10
+
+    def test_nx_directed_gnp_with_fnx_digraph(self):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        g = nx.gnp_random_graph(10, 0.3, seed=42, directed=True, create_using=fnx.DiGraph)
+        assert isinstance(g, fnx.DiGraph)
